extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay held 15-second GC lead over YATES Simon through five-lap technical circuit, YATES won stage sprint
tactical_patterns: HAYTER Ethan mechanical at critical moment forced lone pacing from teammate, revealing depth fragility on compact circuits.
UAE controlled without riding, allowing INEOS to police early breaks protecting their minuscule 15-second GC cushion.
TIBERI Antonio's late positioning move gained two GC places, exploiting bonus seconds vulnerability of favorites in stage sprint.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: defended 15-second lead without teammates visibly working, suggests exceptional positioning autonomy on technical circuits
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: won stage sprint despite team mechanical crisis, strong finishing speed after five technical laps
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: mechanical at decisive moment exposed INEOS lack of depth when Hepburn already in breakaway
rider_signals.TIBERI Antonio: positive: tactical sprint positioning gained two GC places via bonus seconds, mature race-craft for late-stage opportunism
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: dropped in final sprint lost two seconds, vulnerable against pure sprinters after hard circuits
